feed.title: Justin Stone
feed.subtitle: I build practical systems across software, automation, local AI tooling, data workflows, and technical problem-solving.

entry.title: Quality, Then Area, Then Everything Else: Ames Housing with Stacked Ensembles

link.title: Quality, Then Area, Then Everything Else: Ames Housing with Stacked Ensembles

entry.summary: A stacked Ridge + XGBoost + LightGBM ensemble on Ames houses reaches 0.126 RMSLE, beating a Ridge baseline by about 8 percent. The stack weights are unbalanced, and that's fine.

entry.title: The Default Threshold Is the Bug: Credit Card Fraud on the ULB Benchmark

link.title: The Default Threshold Is the Bug: Credit Card Fraud on the ULB Benchmark

entry.summary: A fraud classifier reaches 0.98 ROC-AUC on the famous ULB dataset and still ships a policy most fraud teams would reject. The 0.5 decision threshold is the bug.

entry.title: The California Housing Dataset Is Mostly a Geography Lesson

link.title: The California Housing Dataset Is Mostly a Geography Lesson

entry.summary: A practical look at the California housing prices dataset: what the rows actually mean, why the target is capped, and what this dataset is good for teaching.
